question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(String entityId) path where the SPLITTER uses omitEmptyStrings() and trimResults(), causing a crafted ID like '0..1000' (double dot) to be parsed as a 2-part ID instead of 3-part, defaulting shard from CommonProperties and resolving to a different topic than intended, reorganizing which topic's history is returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Topic domain object's adminKey field returned via findById to determine whether a topic has an admin key set, and by correlating this with the timestampRange, infer when the admin key was added or removed, reorganizing their understanding of the topic's governance history without any special permissions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Topic entity's feeScheduleKey field returned by findById to determine the current fee schedule key, and by comparing responses over time, reconstruct the history of fee schedule key rotations, effectively reorganizing the visible fee governance history of the topic without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Topic entity's feeExemptKeyList field returned by findById to enumerate which keys are exempt from fees, and by polling the endpoint repeatedly, detect changes to the feeExemptKeyList over time, reconstructing the history of fee exemption changes and reorganizing the apparent governance timeline of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Topic entity's submitKey field returned by findById to determine who is authorized to submit messages, and by correlating submitKey changes (via repeated polling) with the timestampRange, reconstruct the history of submit key rotations, reorganizing the apparent authorization history of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById does not validate that the EntityId corresponds to an entity of type TOPIC before querying topicRepository, relying instead on the topic table's contents, causing a scenario where a non-topic entity ID that happens to have a matching row in the topic table (due to backfill migration) returns unexpected historical data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId CACHE's static nature (shared across all requests in the JVM) to perform a cache poisoning attack by making requests that populate the cache with EntityId objects for specific encoded longs, causing subsequent requests from other users to receive cached EntityId objects that map to different shard/realm/num combinations, reorganizing which topic history is returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicRepository's CrudRepository.findById which executes a SELECT on the 'topic' table without any ORDER BY or timestamp filtering, causing it to return the current (latest) topic state even when the user's intent is to query historical state, effectively hiding all prior topic_history records and reorganizing the visible history to show only the present?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId encoding where shard=0 and realm=0 causes encode() to return num directly (the fast path), while shard=0 and realm=1 causes full bit-packing, meaning that '0.0.1000' and '0.1.1000' produce different encoded longs that map to different topic rows, and by exploiting this discrepancy, cause findById to return different topics' histories for what appear to be similar IDs?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the response caching header 'public, max-age=5' on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to serve stale topic data from a CDN or proxy cache for up to 5 seconds after a topic update, causing consumers of the API to see an outdated timestampRange and reorganized historical state that does not reflect the latest transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' CORS header on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to make cross-origin requests from a malicious web page, silently polling the endpoint to track changes in a topic's timestampRange and reconstruct the history of topic updates, reorganizing the apparent transaction history for surveillance purposes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's mapping of entity.timestampRange to the 'timestamp' field in the response, where the 'to' field is null for active topics, to determine that a topic is currently active and has not been deleted, enabling targeted enumeration of active topics whose transaction history could be reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter's regex allowing shard values up to 9999 (4 digits) while the actual SHARD_MASK only allows 1023 (10 bits), causing EntityId.encode() to throw InvalidEntityException for shard values between 1024 and 9999, and using the differential error response to enumerate valid shard ranges and map the network topology for history reorganization attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById throws EntityNotFoundException with the fixed message 'Topic not found' regardless of whether the topic never existed or was deleted, preventing consumers from distinguishing between a topic that never had history and one whose history was deleted, reorganizing the apparent historical record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicRepository extending CrudRepository without any custom query methods, meaning findById always fetches the full Topic entity including adminKey, submitKey, feeScheduleKey, and feeExemptKeyList as byte arrays, and by analyzing these byte arrays across multiple topics, reconstruct key reuse patterns that reveal the historical governance structure of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Topic entity's createdTimestamp field returned by findById to determine the exact consensus timestamp when a topic was created, and by correlating this with the timestampRange lower bound, detect whether the topic was created and immediately updated (indicating a history reorganization event) without any special permissions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(long id) method's behavior where negative longs are valid inputs (e.g., -1 maps to shard=1023, realm=65535, num=274877906943), causing topicRepository.findById to be called with a negative long that may match a topic row inserted with a negative primary key due to a database migration bug, surfacing unintended historical data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicController's independent calls to topicService.findById, entityService.findById, and customFeeService.findById without a distributed lock or transaction, causing a race condition where a topic update transaction commits between the first and second service calls, resulting in a response that mixes historical states from before and after the update, reorganizing the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId CACHE's maximumSize=500000 limit by sending 500,001 unique topic ID requests to fill and then overflow the cache, causing the least-recently-used EntityId to be evicted and re-created on the next request, potentially with a different internal state if CommonProperties.getShard() or getRealm() has changed, reorganizing which topic history is returned for that ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's mapping of topic.createdTimestamp (from the topic table) vs entity.createdTimestamp (from the entity table) where these may differ due to the backfill migration, causing the API response to show a createdTimestamp that does not match the actual first transaction for that topic, reorganizing the apparent creation history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the topic table's timestampRange uses int8range (PostgreSQL range type) and the lower bound is inclusive while the upper bound is exclusive, causing off-by-one ambiguity in the historical window returned by findById, where a topic update at exactly the boundary timestamp appears in both the current and historical records, reorganizing the apparent transaction sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter's parsing of two-part IDs where the ambiguity between 'realm.num' and 'shard.num' is resolved by treating the first segment as realm (not shard), causing findById to look up a topic with realm=X instead of shard=X, returning a completely different topic's history than the user intended to query?
